title: Risk Palate

introduction.subtitle: What risks would you actually accept? A risk menu across five life domains.
introduction.description:
Most people are not uniformly risk-taking or risk-averse — they have a palate. You may gamble with creative risk and recoil at financial risk. This quiz maps where your appetite sits across five domains.

For each scenario, choose what you would actually accept, not what you wish you would.

Use the result as language for reflection and conversation, not as a permanent label. The terms are meant to make patterns easier to notice, compare, and discuss.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is your choice.
introduction.disclaimer: Original instrument. This is not a validated psychometric assessment.

questions.text: Invest a large share of your life savings into your own venture, with no guaranteed return.

conceptHelp.title: Financial Risk: Invest a large share of your life savings into your own venture, with no guaranteed return.
conceptHelp.summary: Financial Risk means comfort with risking money, income, savings, or financial stability for a possible upside. This statement is one concrete signal for that risk domain: "Invest a large share of your life savings into your own venture, with no guaranteed return.".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Self-knowledge items are useful when they help you notice repeated behavior. They become misleading if one answer is treated as a permanent identity or diagnosis.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Think across normal weeks, not one unusually good or bad day. Rate the statement by how often it describes your real preference or behavior.
conceptHelp.example: A high answer means this statement is regularly true for you; a low answer means it is rare, context-specific, or not how you usually operate.

questions.text: Take a significant salary cut for a year to keep your venture alive.

conceptHelp.title: Financial Risk: Take a significant salary cut for a year to keep your venture alive.
conceptHelp.summary: Financial Risk means comfort with risking money, income, savings, or financial stability for a possible upside. This statement is one concrete signal for that risk domain: "Take a significant salary cut for a year to keep your venture alive.".

questions.text: Publicly champion a position many people in your field consider wrong or naive.

conceptHelp.title: Reputational Risk: Publicly champion a position many people in your field consider wrong or naive.
conceptHelp.summary: Reputational Risk means comfort with being visibly judged, criticized, or associated with uncertain work in public. This statement is one concrete signal for that risk domain: "Publicly champion a position many people in your field consider wrong or naive.".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Self-knowledge items are useful when they help you notice repeated behavior. They become misleading if one answer is treated as a permanent identity or diagnosis.

conceptHelp.example: A high answer means this statement is regularly true for you; a low answer means it is rare, context-specific, or not how you usually operate.

questions.text: Attach your full real name to a risky early launch that may fail visibly.

conceptHelp.title: Reputational Risk: Attach your full real name to a risky early launch that may fail visibly.
conceptHelp.summary: Reputational Risk means comfort with being visibly judged, criticized, or associated with uncertain work in public. This statement is one concrete signal for that risk domain: "Attach your full real name to a risky early launch that may fail visibly.".

questions.text: Partner long-term with someone whose values differ sharply from yours on key issues.

conceptHelp.title: Relational Risk: Partner long-term with someone whose values differ sharply from yours on key issues.
conceptHelp.summary: Relational Risk means comfort with putting important relationships, trust, or social bonds at risk for a meaningful possibility. This statement is one concrete signal for that risk domain: "Partner long-term with someone whose values differ sharply from yours on key issues.".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Self-knowledge items are useful when they help you notice repeated behavior. They become misleading if one answer is treated as a permanent identity or diagnosis.

conceptHelp.example: A high answer means this statement is regularly true for you; a low answer means it is rare, context-specific, or not how you usually operate.

questions.text: Ask a close friend to put their own money at risk in your venture.

conceptHelp.title: Relational Risk: Ask a close friend to put their own money at risk in your venture.
conceptHelp.summary: Relational Risk means comfort with putting important relationships, trust, or social bonds at risk for a meaningful possibility. This statement is one concrete signal for that risk domain: "Ask a close friend to put their own money at risk in your venture.".

questions.text: Bet your career on a venture that, if it fails, leaves you with no obvious fallback.

conceptHelp.title: Existential Risk: Bet your career on a venture that, if it fails, leaves you with no obvious fallback.
conceptHelp.summary: Existential Risk means comfort with risks that could reshape identity, career path, safety, or long-term life options. This statement is one concrete signal for that risk domain: "Bet your career on a venture that, if it fails, leaves you with no obvious fallback.".

conceptHelp.example: A high answer means this statement is regularly true for you; a low answer means it is rare, context-specific, or not how you usually operate.

conceptHelp.title: Existential Risk: Move your family across the world for the venture, accepting real relocation risk.

questions.text: Release an original work before it is finished, knowing it may be judged harshly.

conceptHelp.title: Creative Risk: Release an original work before it is finished, knowing it may be judged harshly.
conceptHelp.summary: Creative Risk means comfort with exposing original, unfinished, or unconventional work to judgment. This statement is one concrete signal for that risk domain: "Release an original work before it is finished, knowing it may be judged harshly.".

questions.text: Abandon an established style or method for something genuinely experimental that may not land.

conceptHelp.title: Creative Risk: Abandon an established style or method for something genuinely experimental that may not land.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
